The '''Battle of Nocto''' marked the final engagement between the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire's]] [[Task Force Vengeance|Task Force ''Vengeance'']]. It was the last-ditch attempt of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Wooyou Senn]] to destroy the Alliance [[Telgorn shipyard|shipyard]] in the system with the meager remainders of his task force.

==The battle==
Having raided and worn down [[Wooyou Senn|Senn's]] [[Task Force Vengeance|Task Force ''Vengeance'']] during the past few weeks, the Alliance did not expect the admiral to know where the vital shipyard ''[[Calenz]]'' was located.

[[File:Avenger_squadron_attack_Calenz.jpg|thumb|left|200px|[[Avenger Squadron]] attacks the ''Calenz''.]]
It came as a big surprise to the Rebel forces in the [[Airam sector/Legends|Airam sector]] when the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Vengeance (Executor-class)|''Vengeance'']], flanked by the Imperial Star Destroyer [[Rage (Imperial-class)|''Rage'']], dropped out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] in the [[Nocto system/Legends|Nocto system]]. Due to a miscalculation on Senn's part (or possible inexact intelligence on the precise location of the facility) both [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] reverted to [[Realspace/Legends|realspace]] well beyond weapons range, which bought the Rebels crucial time to scramble both [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue]] and [[Green Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Green Squadrons]].

Only minutes after the starfighter launch, the ''Rage'' corrected Senn's tactical mishap by performing a [[Precision hyperspace jump|microjump]], effectively ending up on the opposite side of ''Calenz'' and forcing the Rebel starfighter squadrons to split up in order to defend the facility.<ref name="X Wing vs. TIE Fighter" />

[[MC80 Liberty Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|Calamari Cruiser]] [[Liberty/Legends|''Liberty'']] reacted to the distress signal from ''Calenz'' and reached the system soon enough to position itself between Senn's Star Dreadnought and the shipyard.<ref name="X Wing vs. TIE Fighter" /> However, Rogue and Green Squadrons were effectively tied up by the Imperial fighter defenses, and a few [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bombers]] managed to unleash their payload upon the shipyard. The Imperial attackers dealt enough damage that ''Calenz'' was seriously damaged or destroyed before the Rebels could rally to fight off the bomber swarms.<ref name="X Wing vs. TIE Fighter" />

Simultaneously, the Super Star Destroyer launched a squadron of [[Gamma-class ATR-6 assault transport]]s armed with [[Proton rocket/Legends|heavy rockets]]. Senn intended to destroy, once and for all, the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] warship that had plagued his sector operations.

[[File:Corv_Thunder2.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The ''Thunder'' intends on ramming the SSD [[Vengeance (Executor-class)|''Vengeance'']].]]
Against great odds, Green Squadron, in conjunction with [[Ramming/Legends|suicide attacks]] by three allied [[Airam (group)|Airam]] [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Dreadnaught''-class cruisers]], managed to knock out the Super Star Destroyer's bridge [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]], at which point the [[Airam sector/Legends|Airam]] [[CR90 corvette/Legends|corvette]] [[Thunder (CR90 corvette)|''Thunder'']], loaded to bursting with [[Proton bomb/Legends|space bombs]], dropped out of hyperspace with the intention of ramming the ''Vengeance''{{'s}} bridge—a task in which it succeeded, despite taking heavy damage at the hands of the Star Dreadnought's gunners, killing Admiral Senn on the bridge and heavily damaging the vessel.<ref name="X Wing vs. TIE Fighter" /> The suicide run left only a small amount of armor to break through for Green Squadron.<ref name="X Wing vs. TIE Fighter" />

Both the ''Vengeance'' and the ''Rage'' are believed to have been destroyed at the hands of the Rebel forces, but the Alliance shipyard was also lost.<ref name="X Wing vs. TIE Fighter" />

==Aftermath==
[[File:SSD_Vengeance_explosion.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The demise of ''Vengeance'']]
The complete annihilation of [[Task Force Vengeance|Task Force ''Vengeance'''s]] remainders was a significant blow to the Galactic Empire. The Empire had lost at least twenty-five capital ships over the past several weeks, including most or all of their starfighter complements and the reinforcement fleet sent to assist Senn.<ref name="X Wing vs. TIE Fighter" />

The remoteness of the [[Airam sector/Legends|Airam sector]] meant that the Empire was not likely to send another expedition force of significant size to pacify it, thus ensuring the new freedom of the Airam and a safe haven for the Rebel Alliance, where they could regroup and rebuild their strength. However, it is uncertain whether the Rebels maintained their hard-fought stronghold in the Airam sector, as the shipyard ''Calenz'' was regarded as mission-critical by Alliance forces during the Battle of Nocto. It is possible that its destruction, along with the heavy damage done to the ''Liberty'' and the destruction of four Airam [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]], forced the Rebels to withdraw and regroup elsewhere, making the [[Balance of Power in the Airam Sector|Airam campaign]] largely a wash.

[[File:Calenz_destroyed.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Telgorn shipyard ''[[Calenz]]'' is destroyed.]]
At any rate, the Airam sector is not mentioned beyond the Battle of Nocto as a stronghold for either the Rebel Alliance or the Galactic Empire, nor is the status of the Airam Clans after its bloody events currently known.<ref name="X Wing vs. TIE Fighter" />

==Behind the scenes==
The actual canon outcome of the Battle of Nocto is disputed, because the two [[Star Wars: X-Wing vs. TIE Fighter: Balance of Power Campaigns|''Balance of Power'']] campaigns (for the Empire and the Rebellion, respectively) have alternate endings depending on which side the player is on. One interpretation is to assume that all missions and cutscenes from both campaigns are canonical, and that all mission objectives were achieved (as so to advance the storyline of the campaign on to the next mission, and so on), rendering all engagements' outcomes known with the exception of the Battle of Nocto. In the game itself, it is either the ''Vengeance'' or the shipyard which is destroyed by the player's forces, with the other required by mission goals to survive.

Following the standards adopted for handling the rest of the missions, this article assumes that both sides succeeded in destroying their primary targets, which would result in a draw. However, text seen by the player after the Imperial victory includes a formal statement that Rogue Squadron was wholly eradicated, thus clashing with canon. Accounting for the destruction of most of the attacking force and the survival of Rogue Squadron, the outcome of the Battle of Nocto thus appears more akin to a Pyrrhic victory for the Rebel Alliance. Considering the stated importance of the shipyard, it is possible that the battle was a tactical stalemate, but the defeat of Task Force ''Vengeance'' by an outgunned Rebel/Airam fleet renders it a strategic triumph for the Rebellion.
